bets.name: OG-only Human-State Assignment Teacher
bets.worldview: The public-sensor teacher can be replaced by personal/cohort/time human-state consistency.
bets.adapter_move: Train a row-target support teacher from OG personal/cohort/time masks, then feed it into the existing invariant decoder.
bets.why_big: If it works, HS-JEPA becomes a portable architecture rather than a public-sensor case study.
bets.kill_criterion: Cell orientation remains high but row assignment stays near random under subject/time stress.

bets.name: Listener-Invariant Contrastive Decoder
bets.worldview: A correction should be selected by agreement between listener responsibility and invariant energy, not public utility alone.
bets.adapter_move: Score candidate row-target actions by listener gain minus route-energy toxicity under random feasible nulls.
bets.why_big: This could move beyond the current S2 bridge into a general action-health decoder.
bets.kill_criterion: Listener gain and invariant energy remain anti-correlated on strong candidates.

bets.name: Private-Safe Toxicity Field
bets.worldview: The plateau comes from actions that help public-like rows but poison private-like rows.
bets.adapter_move: Use failed public sensors, null bundles, and cohort/time stress to learn a toxicity veto before submission packaging.
bets.why_big: A true toxicity field can preserve the 0.567 gain while reducing private-risk and maybe exposing larger safe moves.
bets.kill_criterion: Toxicity score only recovers known public failures and does not separate local nulls.

bets.name: Cross-Listener Human-State Transport
bets.worldview: Subjective Q and objective S labels are different listeners of one human state, not separate tasks.
bets.adapter_move: Move actions only when Q-listener and S-listener state transitions are mutually consistent.
bets.why_big: This attacks the current weakness that Q and S decoders are mostly separated.
bets.kill_criterion: Q-S bridge actions fail null tests or replicate the already-killed subjective-shadow bridge.
